query: Use /consultant to turn the approval-inbox notes in this repo into an implementation-ready plan. Ask one recommended-option question at a time and stop at handoff.
Invoke the Consultant skill for this rough shift-handoff feature, keep persistent state, and run the adversarial review only after I approve the plan.
/consultant continue the existing harbor-kiosk consultation at ~/.consultant/harbor-kiosk from its saved next question.
Please use Consultant as the named workflow to map the knowns and unknowns for this offline export idea, resolve them with me, and produce plan.md.
Activate /consultant for this dashboard redesign. Preserve my taste decisions, get a fresh reviewer to challenge the plan, and give me the builder handoff.
Run the Consultant skill on the current territory, but do not resume any old consultation and do not implement anything.
/consultant resume the named access-review slug, incorporate the new audit constraint, re-gate the adversarial review, and stop when the plan is approved.
I explicitly want the Consultant skill here: inspect the repository facts first, ask me the highest-leverage product questions, and hand off the reviewed plan.
Call consultant for this ambiguous permissions workflow and use a taste reference if the interaction choice is hard to describe in words.
Use /consultant to finish this client consultation, including a maximum of five blocker-review rounds and a safe copy-paste prompt for the implementation agent.
Help me plan an approval inbox. Ask a few useful questions, write a concise plan, and then implement it in this repository.
Review the existing plan.md for technical blockers and leave inline feedback, but do not start a consultation.
Act like a product consultant and brainstorm three positioning options for our fictional analytics launch.
Continue editing the project plan at docs/migration-plan.md from the TODO section; no separate consultation state is needed.
Implement the approved plan in ~/.consultant/offline-export/plan.md and run the relevant tests.
Turn these meeting notes into a PRD with goals, user stories, acceptance criteria, and open questions.
Use /automake to improve this onboarding flow with an independent evaluator and a three-iteration ratchet.
Explain the four known/unknown quadrants and give me a filled example for a product discovery workshop.
Do not invoke the Consultant skill. Just answer whether this plan should include pagination in one paragraph.
Our consulting firm needs a cleaner invoice template for external consultants and subcontractors.
